summary: First iteration: comprehensive backend + frontend testing for RapidXT Hisaab. 30/30 backend pytest passing after fixing a KeyError bug in owner_routes. Frontend admin and owner flows pass after renaming missing testid.

minor.issue: Root /api/ returns 404 because @api.get('/') is defined AFTER app.include_router(api). Cosmetic only.

action_items: (Optional) Move @api.get('/') route definition above app.include_router(api) so /api/ root works.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED by tester in /app/backend/owner_routes.py: list_projects() and get_project() crashed with KeyError: '_id' because _strip_id(p) mutates p to remove _id BEFORE the code accessed p['_id'] for stage history lookup and _project_summary(). Refactored to capture pid_local / summary before stripping.
owner_routes.py is 862 lines long — exceeds 700-line guideline; recommend splitting into modules (projects, transactions, stages, materials, documents, reports).
send_email_smtp uses positional smtplib args — works, but SMTP test endpoint POST /api/admin/smtp/test passes raw dict (no Pydantic model). Recommend a TestSmtpIn model.
Backend imports `LoginIn` etc. via relative-less imports (`from models import ...`); fine because backend/ is the cwd via supervisor, but consider explicit package.
Cookies set with samesite='none' + secure=True is correct for cross-site preview env, but breaks in pure local http; acceptable in this environment.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminPanel.jsx (renamed owner-name-input -> biz-owner-name-input testid to match spec)
test_credentials: Super admin: admin@rapidxt.com / Admin@123. Test owners are auto-created during pytest run with TEST_Biz_* prefix and during UI test as ui_owner_<timestamp>@example.com / Owner@123.
seed_data_creation: Pytest fixtures create 2 active businesses (TEST_Biz_A_*, TEST_Biz_B_*), 1 password-reset biz, 1 expired biz, plus clients/projects/transactions/stages/materials/reminders within biz_a. UI test created one additional UI_Biz_<ts> business.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 30 backend pytest tests pass against the live preview URL. Tested: auth (admin/owner/forgot), super-admin CRUD on businesses, SMTP, role/plan guards, multi-tenant isolation between two fresh businesses, projects (create+list+detail with summary nesting + flat keys), transactions (filter & summary effect), extra work (only Approved counts toward revised_value), stages reorder + project stage history, materials cost aggregation, reminders, dashboard, FY profit, receivables/payables reports, image upload with PIL compression. UI verified: admin login -> /admin, add-customer dialog with biz-owner-name-input testid (renamed), customer table refresh, /admin/smtp page, owner login -> /dashboard with no console/visible errors. backend_test.py is the canonical regression suite for future runs.
rca of the issue: Root cause of project list/detail 500s: in owner_routes.py, _strip_id(doc) is in-place: it pops '_id' and adds 'id'. The list_projects loop called _strip_id(p) first and then immediately tried p['_id'] for stage lookup and _project_summary(p) which itself reads project['_id']. Fix: capture the id and call _project_summary with a dict containing _id, before/after stripping. Same root cause in get_project (already-stripped p passed to _project_summary). Reproduction: login as owner with at least 1 project and call GET /api/projects or GET /api/projects/{id} -> 500 KeyError: '_id'. Mitigation applied: refactor to preserve _id locally for downstream calls.
